items.what_it_depicts: Autocar's editorial photograph of the McLaren F1 from Steve Cropley's May 11, 1994 full test.
items.why_add: Primary source editorial photograph; anchors the exhibit's main citation.
items.true_original_source: Autocar magazine, May 11 1994, Cropley full test
items.chicago_citation: Autocar, McLaren F1 Road-Test Photograph, May 11 1994, via autocar.co.uk archives.

items.what_it_depicts: Autocar interior / central-driving-position photograph of the F1.
items.why_add: Shows the central driving position feature Cropley called out in the test.

items.what_it_depicts: Car Magazine June 1994 cover featuring the McLaren F1 — the secondary source on the exhibit's bibliography.
items.why_add: Period magazine cover directly from the cited secondary source.
items.chicago_citation: Car Magazine, June 1994 cover, McLaren F1 supercar test.

items.what_it_depicts: First spread of Car Magazine's June 1994 original McLaren F1 road test.
items.why_add: Period magazine spread from the exhibit's cited secondary source.
items.chicago_citation: Car Magazine, June 1994 McLaren F1 road-test, first spread.

items.what_it_depicts: Second spread of Car Magazine's June 1994 F1 test — interior architecture.
items.why_add: Documents the central-driver-seat layout from period press.
items.chicago_citation: Car Magazine, June 1994 McLaren F1 road-test, second spread.

items.what_it_depicts: Third Car Magazine June 1994 spread showing the BMW S70/2 V12 engine bay and gold-foil heat shield.
items.why_add: Visual documentation of the engine bay Cropley and CAR's writers praised in period.
items.chicago_citation: Car Magazine, June 1994 McLaren F1 road-test, engine bay spread.

items.what_it_depicts: 28-year-old Elon Musk taking delivery of his silver McLaren F1 (chassis 067) in 1999.
items.why_add: The archetypal dot-com supercar purchase; Musk crashed it uninsured the following year giving Peter Thiel a ride.
items.true_original_source: CNN news footage, 1999, via Motor1 / InsideEVs
items.chicago_citation: CNN archival footage, Elon Musk Takes Delivery of His McLaren F1, 1999.

items.what_it_depicts: Musk beside the F1 during the 1999 delivery.
items.why_add: Second still from the delivery footage; establishes the moment.
items.chicago_citation: CNN archival footage, Elon Musk Receives McLaren F1, 1999.

items.what_it_depicts: The winning #59 Kokusai Kaihatsu Yamaichi McLaren F1 GTR team (Dalmas, Sekiya, Lehto) on the podium at the 1995 Le Mans 24 Hours.
items.why_add: The upset-victory podium shot; F1's factory backed up Cropley's 'world's greatest supercar' framing with an outright Le Mans win in its first attempt.
items.true_original_source: Martin Lee Flickr archive via Wikimedia Commons
items.chicago_citation: Martin Lee, Winning McLaren F1 GTR #59 Team on the Podium at Le Mans 1995, June 11 1995, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 2.0.

items.what_it_depicts: The winning #59 F1 GTR at the Ford Chicane, 1995 Le Mans.
items.why_add: In-race action of the eventual winner; the car captured in competition, not museum-static.
items.true_original_source: Martin Lee Flickr archive via Wikimedia Commons
items.chicago_citation: Martin Lee, Winning McLaren F1 GTR #59 at Ford Chicane, Le Mans 1995, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 2.0.

items.what_it_depicts: The 3rd-place #51 F1 GTR team (Wallace, Derek Bell, Justin Bell) on the 1995 Le Mans podium.
items.why_add: Three McLaren F1 GTRs finished in the top five at Le Mans 1995; documents the team-wide dominance.
items.true_original_source: Martin Lee Flickr archive via Wikimedia Commons
items.chicago_citation: Martin Lee, 3rd Place McLaren F1 GTR #51 Team on Podium, Le Mans 1995, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 2.0.

items.what_it_depicts: All three podium finishers at the 1995 Le Mans 24 Hours — McLaren F1 GTR swept 1st and 3rd.
items.why_add: Composite podium image documenting the historic McLaren result.
items.true_original_source: Martin Lee Flickr archive via Wikimedia Commons
items.chicago_citation: Martin Lee, 1st/2nd/3rd Place Finishers at Le Mans 1995 Podium, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 2.0.

items.what_it_depicts: The 1995 Le Mans-winning McLaren F1 GTR (#01R) on display at Goodwood Festival of Speed.
items.why_add: The actual winning chassis, preserved and displayed.
items.chicago_citation: Spycatcher58, 1995 Le Mans Winning McLaren F1 GTR at Goodwood Festival of Speed, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0.

items.what_it_depicts: Gordon Murray — the F1's designer — in the paddock at the 1996 Le Mans, one year after his car's outright win.
items.why_add: The designer portrait; shows Murray as a racing-paddock figure, not just a draftsman.
items.true_original_source: Martin Lee Flickr archive via Wikimedia Commons
items.chicago_citation: Martin Lee, Gordon Murray in the Paddock at the 1996 Le Mans, 1996, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 2.0.

items.what_it_depicts: Open engine bay of the McLaren F1 LM showing the BMW S70/2 V12 and the gold-foil heat-shield lining.
items.why_add: The gold-foil engine bay is a cultural talking point; period engineering detail.
items.chicago_citation: Chelsea Jay, 1995 McLaren F1 LM Engine Bay, 2018, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0.
